ogTitle: Fifteen Mile vs Fig Tree Pocket: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Fifteen Mile and Fig Tree Pocket.
heading: Fifteen Mile vs Fig Tree Pocket — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Fifteen Mile, Queensland and Fig Tree Pocket, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Fifteen Mile has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Fig Tree Pocket (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Fifteen Mile or Fig Tree Pocket safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Fifteen Mile has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Fig Tree Pocket scores 51/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Fifteen Mile scores 34/100 for bushfire risk. Fig Tree Pocket scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Fifteen Mile or Fig Tree Pocket?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Fifteen Mile has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.4% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
